title: Briam

overview.description: A traditional Greek tray bake of seasonal vegetables slowly roasted in olive oil, tomatoes and herbs until tender and lightly caramelised.

method: Preheat the oven to 170°C fan (190°C conventional).
Lightly salt the aubergine slices and leave for 20 minutes.
Pat the aubergine dry with kitchen paper.
Layer the potatoes, onions, aubergines and courgettes in a large roasting dish.
Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Cover loosely with foil and bake for 45 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for a further 40–50 minutes until the vegetables are tender and beginning to caramelise.
Scatter with the fresh parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Briam is Greece's version of the many Mediterranean vegetable bakes that celebrate seasonal produce and generous olive oil.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How slow roasting concentrates flavour, caramelises vegetables and creates a rich sauce with very little effort.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the best examples in the cookbook of how simple pantry vegetables can become a complete and deeply satisfying meal.
chapman-tips: Salting the aubergine improves both texture and flavour.
Don't rush the cooking—the caramelisation is what makes the dish special.
Good olive oil makes a noticeable difference here.
Serve with crusty bread to soak up the tomato and olive oil juices.
notes.leftovers: Excellent the next day, either hot or at room temperature.
